text: Why must variables be bound in a quantified statement to form a proposition?
answer: To fix variables to the scope of a quantifier
a: To ensure all variables are integers
b: To limit the domain to positive numbers
c: To fix variables to the scope of a quantifier
d: To exclude logical operators from the statement

text: Which quantifier expresses "There exists a positive integer that is prime and even"?
How does the truth value of \(\forall x (P(x) \rightarrow Q(x))\) depend on counterexamples?
answer: It is false if at least one counterexample exists
a: It is true if at least one counterexample exists
b: It is false if at least one counterexample exists
c: It is true regardless of counterexamples
d: It depends only on existential quantifiers

text: Which expression correctly translates "Every new employee met the deadline"?
Fill in the blank: The scope of a quantifier is the part of the logical expression to which the quantifier _____.
Why is \(\exists x (S(x) \lor P(x))\) not logically equivalent to "Someone was sick and came to the party"?
answer: Because \(\exists x (S(x) \lor P(x))\) allows either condition to be true
a: Because \(\lor\) means both conditions must be true
b: Because \(\exists x (S(x) \lor P(x))\) allows either condition to be true
c: Because \(\exists\) quantifier cannot be used with \(\lor\)

text: Which is true about variable names in quantified statements?
answer: Different variable names can be used if consistently substituted within quantifier scope
a: Variable names cannot be changed once assigned
b: Different variable names can be used if consistently substituted within quantifier scope
c: Using the same variable name in different quantifiers causes errors
d: Variable names must be single letters only

text: Fill in the blank: The predicate \(P(x)\) with variable \(x\) free is called a/an _____ variable.
How does the expression \(\forall x (P(x) \land Q(y))\) differ from \(\forall x (P(x) \land Q(x))\) in terms of proposition status?
answer: The first has a free variable and is not a proposition
b: The first is a proposition; the second is not
c: The first has a free variable and is not a proposition
d: Both have free variables and are not propositions

text: A variable _____ in a predicate is bound if it is attached to a quantifier.
The scope of a quantifier includes _____.
answer: only the predicate it applies to
b: only the predicate it applies to

text: Which quantifier corresponds to "There exists" in logic?
The statement "All prime numbers are odd" translates logically to _____.
A statement with free variables is _____.

answer: There is at least one x for which P(x) and Q(x) are true
a: For every x, P(x) and Q(x) are true
b: There is at least one x for which P(x) and Q(x) are true
c: P(x) and Q(x) are true for no x
d: For every x, either P(x) or Q(x) is true

text: Which logical expression matches "Every new employee met the deadline"?
Variable names in quantified statements can be changed if _____.
answer: the substitution is consistent within the quantifier's scope
b: the substitution is consistent within the quantifier's scope
c: the variable is only in one predicate

text: The proposition \(\exists x (P(x) \lor Q(x))\) is true if _____.
answer: at least one x satisfies P(x) or Q(x)
a: no x satisfies P(x) or Q(x)
b: at least one x satisfies P(x) or Q(x)
c: all x satisfy both P(x) and Q(x)
d: all x satisfy neither P(x) nor Q(x)
